title: China-to-Mexico Solar Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: Product NOM Safety Certification — Approved Certification Body Route
rows.target_requirement: Where a grid-tied PV inverter is classified within a Mexican electrical-product NOM scope, import and sale normally require conformity assessment by an accredited and approved certification body. NYCE and ANCE are common Mexican certification bodies for electrical and electronic NOM work, but the legally relevant point is that the body must be accredited and approved for the exact NOM and product scope. The applicable product NOM can depend on voltage, function, tariff classification, bundled equipment, and whether the inverter is imported as a standalone product or as part of a project system.
target_standards: Ley de Infraestructura de la Calidad — conformity assessment and accredited/approved bodies
Applicable electrical-product NOMs, including NOM-003-SCFI where the product scope applies
Certification body approval scope, commonly through NYCE or ANCE for relevant electrical/electronic NOMs
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese CCC, CQC, CGC, IEC CB, CE, or EN/IEC test reports are useful evidence but do not by themselves authorize NOM marking, customs release under a NOM requirement, or sale in Mexico. A Mexican certification body may reuse selected test data only if its procedure and the applicable NOM permit it.
china_standards: CCC — China Compulsory Certification, not a Mexican NOM certificate
CQC/CGC voluntary certifications — supportive evidence only
IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 CB reports — possible technical input, not NOM approval
rows.gap: The importer must classify the inverter against Mexican NOM scopes and obtain a Mexican certificate when a product NOM applies. Do not assume that IEC 62109, UL 1741, CE, or Chinese certificates are accepted as substitutes for NOM certification.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the inverter falls within an applicable Mexican product NOM scope; certification must come from a body accredited and approved for that scope.
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or non-equivalent for Mexican entry — China-side certificates may support review but do not grant NOM certification.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Informational only. Confirm the exact NOM scope and use an approved certification body such as NYCE or ANCE only where its approval covers that NOM and inverter scope.

source.publisher: Diario Oficial de la Federacion / Secretaria de Economia

rows.requirement: Electrical Product Safety NOMs — Scope Check for Inverters
rows.target_requirement: NOM-003-SCFI is the common Mexican electrical-product safety NOM for many electrical products, but PV inverter applicability is a classification question rather than a universal statement. A broker, certification body, or Mexican compliance professional should confirm the tariff code, product description, rated electrical characteristics, accessories, and any specific NOM exclusions or alternative NOMs before shipment.
target_standards: NOM-003-SCFI — electrical product safety, where product scope applies
NOMs listed in Mexican import and conformity-assessment rules for the applicable tariff classification
rows.china_equivalent: China-side inverter safety files are commonly IEC 62109, GB/T 37408, CQC, CGC, or CB Scheme reports. These may help a Mexican certification body understand safety design, but they do not answer the Mexican tariff/NOM applicability question.
rows.gap: The practical gap is classification. A technically strong IEC/EN file still fails the import process if the NOM certificate, label, holder, model family, or customs documentation does not match the Mexican NOM requirement applied to the tariff line.

mandatory_status.target: Conditional mandatory — required when the applicable tariff/product classification triggers NOM-003-SCFI or another product NOM.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — Chinese product classification and certificates do not determine Mexican NOM applicability.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Informational only. Perform a Mexico-specific NOM scope and tariff check before shipment; do not rely on a generic IEC or China certificate as proof of Mexican product NOM compliance.

source.publisher: Diario Oficial de la Federacion / Secretaria de Economia

rows.target_requirement: CONUEE administers Mexico's energy-efficiency NOM program. As of this dataset version, grid-tied PV inverters should be screened against the current CONUEE NOM list rather than assumed to have a dedicated inverter efficiency NOM. If the inverter is bundled with covered equipment, an external power supply, controls, standby functions, or other regulated components, a separate energy-efficiency NOM may be triggered.
target_standards: CONUEE current energy-efficiency NOM list
Applicable NOM-ENER requirements where the imported configuration includes covered equipment or components
rows.china_equivalent: China efficiency claims, CEC-style inverter efficiency data, IEC efficiency measurements, or CGC/CQC performance certificates can support product marketing and engineering review, but they do not determine whether a Mexican NOM-ENER applies.
china_standards: CGC/CQC voluntary PV inverter performance certification
IEC efficiency measurement evidence where available
rows.gap: The compliance gap is a scope-screening task. The importer should document why no CONUEE NOM applies to the standalone inverter, or identify and certify any covered bundled component before customs entry.

mandatory_status.target: Conditional mandatory — energy-efficiency NOM compliance is mandatory when a listed CONUEE NOM covers the product or bundled component.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — Chinese efficiency or performance certificates do not replace Mexican NOM-ENER determinations.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Informational only. Include a CONUEE NOM scope check in the import file even if no dedicated grid-tied inverter efficiency NOM is found.

rows.requirement: Distributed Generation Interconnection — CRE RES Framework and CFE Process
rows.target_requirement: Mexico's distributed-generation (GD) interconnection route is governed by CRE administrative provisions and implemented through CFE interconnection procedures for generators below the 0.7 MW distributed-generation threshold. A grid-tied PV inverter must satisfy the project-specific CFE connection review, metering arrangement, protection settings, anti-islanding behavior, and commissioning evidence before export to the grid is accepted. This grid approval is separate from NOM product certification and from NOM-001-SEDE installation compliance.
target_standards: CRE Resolucion RES/142/2017 and related distributed generation administrative provisions
CRE DACG Apr 2026 / LESE 2025 distributed-generation threshold: below 0.7 MW
CFE manual or process for interconnection of generation plants below the distributed-generation threshold
Project-specific CFE interconnection contract, metering, and commissioning requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid evidence under GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, State Grid, or China Southern Power Grid procedures is not accepted as Mexican CFE interconnection approval. It may help engineering review, but Mexican grid settings and CFE forms must be completed separately.
rows.gap: The largest technical gap is usually firmware and evidence for Mexican grid settings: voltage and frequency trip points, reconnection timing, anti-islanding, reactive-power behavior, and commissioning records must match CFE/CRE expectations rather than China domestic settings.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid export or parallel operation where the project connects under the distributed-generation framework; CFE interconnection acceptance is project-specific.
mandatory_status.cn: Not transferable — China grid-code compliance does not authorize CFE interconnection.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Informational only. Treat CRE/CFE interconnection as a separate project approval gate after product NOM and installation compliance are addressed.

source.publisher: Diario Oficial de la Federacion / Comision Reguladora de Energia

rows.requirement: CFE Interconnection Manual — Sub-0.7 MW Generation Projects
rows.target_requirement: For distributed-generation projects below the 0.7 MW threshold, CFE documentation and the official interconnection process set the application, technical study, contract, meter, inspection, and commissioning sequence. The inverter model and settings must support the connection mode requested by the customer and must be documented in the CFE file.
target_standards: CRE DACG Apr 2026 / LESE 2025 distributed-generation threshold: below 0.7 MW
Manual de interconexion de centrales de generacion with CFE process for distributed-generation projects
CFE distribution interconnection forms, contract, and metering process
rows.china_equivalent: China project acceptance files may include utility commissioning documents and settings sheets, but Mexican projects must follow the CFE application and contract path. A China utility acceptance record cannot replace the CFE meter and interconnection contract sequence.
china_standards: State Grid distributed PV interconnection procedures
China Southern Power Grid distributed PV interconnection procedures
rows.gap: A product can be importable but still unable to energize until the CFE process is closed. Plan for model documentation, single-line diagrams, protection settings, labels, and commissioning evidence in Spanish for the CFE file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where CFE interconnection is required for the project; the process is project-specific rather than a transferable product certificate.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — Chinese project interconnection evidence does not close the CFE process.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Informational only. CFE interconnection is a project workflow; keep it separate from factory product certification and plan documentation before shipment.

source.publisher: Gobierno de Mexico / Secretaria de Energia

rows.requirement: IEC/EN Test Evidence — Useful Input, Not Mexican Approval
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62109-1/-2 safety reports, IEC 62116 anti-islanding reports, IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, EN 50549 reports, UL 1741/IEEE 1547 files, and CB Scheme certificates can help Mexican certification bodies, engineers, and CFE reviewers understand the inverter's design and grid behavior. They do not replace Mexican NOM certification, NOM-001-SEDE installation compliance, CRE/CFE interconnection, Spanish documentation, or any required NOM/energy-efficiency certificate.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 — PV power converter safety evidence
IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 — anti-islanding and utility-interface evidence
EN 50549, UL 1741, IEEE 1547 — possible comparative grid evidence, not Mexican legal approval
Mexican NOM and CRE/CFE requirements remain controlling for market entry and interconnection
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese exporters often already hold IEC, EN, UL, CB, CQC, CGC, or GB/T reports for other markets. These should be organized as supporting evidence for the Mexican certification body and project engineer, with a separate delta matrix showing Mexican NOM, CFE, and Spanish-label/documentation gaps.
rows.gap: The evidence gap is translation and jurisdictional acceptance: international reports must be mapped into Mexican NOM clauses, certification-body procedures, CFE grid settings, and Spanish documentation rather than presented as automatic equivalence.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary supporting evidence unless incorporated by a Mexican NOM, certification-body procedure, CFE requirement, or project contract.
mandatory_status.cn: Common supporting evidence — useful but not sufficient for Mexican approval.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Informational only. Use IEC/EN/UL/GB evidence as a technical bridge, then close the Mexican NOM, CRE/CFE, CONUEE, and Spanish-documentation gates separately.

source.publisher: Gobierno de Mexico / Secretaria de Economia

rows.target_requirement: Mexico treats the installed PV system and its wiring, protection, grounding, disconnecting means, and labeling through NOM-001-SEDE for electrical installations. For a grid-tied PV inverter, the product certificate alone does not approve the installation: the installer and project owner must ensure the inverter is selected, wired, protected, marked, and commissioned consistently with the installation NOM and the local authority or utility acceptance process.
target_standards: NOM-001-SEDE, Instalaciones electricas (utilizacion)
Applicable CFE interconnection and metering requirements for distributed generation
rows.china_equivalent: China installation and grid-connection evidence may include GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, design drawings, and State Grid or China Southern Power Grid acceptance documents. These documents can support engineering review but do not replace Mexican installation compliance under NOM-001-SEDE.
china_standards: GB/T 19964 — PV power station grid connection
GB/T 37408 — photovoltaic grid-connected inverter technical requirements
NB/T 32004 — grid-connected PV inverter technical specification
rows.gap: The gap is installation acceptance, not only product testing. A China type-test file does not prove Mexican wiring methods, disconnect locations, grounding, warning labels, conductor sizing, or CFE metering/interconnection details.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for electrical installations where NOM-001-SEDE applies; product certification and utility interconnection remain separate checks.
mandatory_status.cn: Not transferable — Chinese installation or utility acceptance evidence does not grant Mexican installation acceptance.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Informational only. Treat NOM-001-SEDE as the installation gate for the Mexican PV system, separate from the inverter's product NOM certificate and separate from CFE interconnection approval.

source.publisher: Diario Oficial de la Federacion / Secretaria de Energia
